reasoning: There are 64 players in the single-elimination 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship, giving an average win probability of roughly 1.56% per player (1/64). While John Smith is a reputable heads-up player (runner-up twice in the WSOP $10K heads-up), the field contains top-tier professional high-rollers like Jason Koon and Stephen Chidwick, as well as many other skilled players. This suggests his odds of winning six consecutive matches are likely around or slightly above average for the field. There is a mention of a leaked winner via prediction markets, but no evidence confirms it is John Smith. Therefore, his chances remain close to the base rate of ~1.5%-2.0%. Since the lowest valid bound in most prediction contexts for 64-player tournaments is around 2%, setting the estimate to 0.02 is the most mathematically robust choice.
